description: En-D2 — a composite state-child (§51.0.Q.1) whose inner nested <engine> uses inside-opener `:`-shorthand state-children (`<Exploring rule=.Battle : "...">`) compiles clean. Pre-fix, each uppercase `:`-shorthand child bumped the state-child depth counter in the nested-engine closer scan (findEngineCloser / findStateChildCloser PascalCase branch) — but a `:`-shorthand opener is self-terminating (no closer), so the phantom depth never popped, overran the inner <engine> boundary, and flattened the inner children UP into the OUTER engine. The outer then saw Exploring/Battle as its own state-children (invalid AppMode variants) and lost Playing's real content, firing false E-ENGINE-STATE-CHILD-MISSING (outer) + E-ENGINE-STATE-CHILD-INVALID-VARIANT / E-ENGINE-RULE-INVALID-VARIANT (inner). Inner bare-body and inner self-closing children were unaffected; the trigger was a single inner `:`-shorthand child.
rationale: §51.0.Q.1: the nested <engine> in a composite state-child has FULL engine semantics, including §51.0.I `:`-shorthand state-children. The nested-engine boundary scan must treat an uppercase `:`-shorthand opener as self-terminating (no closer), exactly as the §4.14 lowercase-HTML branch already does, so the inner children stay attributed to the inner engine.
